steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Recall the radians-to-degrees conversion factor
steps.content: To convert from radians to degrees, multiply by $\frac{180^{\circ}}{\pi}$, since $\pi$ radians equals $180^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Apply the conversion formula
steps.content:
Multiply the radian measure by the conversion factor:
$$93 \cdot 2\pi \times \frac{180^{\circ}}{\pi}$$

steps.title: Cancel $\pi$ and compute the result
steps.content:
The $\pi$ terms cancel, leaving:
$$93 \cdot 2 \times 180^{\circ} = 93 \cdot 360^{\circ}$$
That confirms B is correct.
